ratification_note: Outcome events are PLAIN PAST TENSE (DR-2 override). Cause tags are machine-readable only — never rendered raw (amendment 2). Client logs each posted outcome event + region state via createLogger (amendment 3). INV-PCO: never read ui-state as a resource oracle. No terminal dead-ends: every failure has a tested retry path.
scope_boundary: The write model moves from server-side ui-state actors to client-driven optimistic write-through + outcome reports. auth-proxy becomes the sole WorkOS boundary + sole AUTH_MODE reader (org-create interception); backend becomes a pure resource store; ui-state becomes a zero-egress presentation-state coordinator; ui/ gains the onboarding-driver. Org + a single automatic default project ONLY. ui/ only (NOT frontend/). NON-GOALS: project naming during onboarding, member invites, multi-org, changing the /state transport (ADR-046 unchanged).
acceptance_suite: tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/ — REWORKED IN PLACE (AR-6; NOT a new suite directory). Own pyproject.toml + venv; run: cd tests/acceptance/org-onboarding && uv run --no-project pytest. NOT run by the refinery --auto gate — RED-by-design until DELIVER builds the feature; skips (never fails) when the compose stack is unreachable.

walking_skeleton.strategy: C (real local / @real_io) — real compose stack (auth-proxy + backend + ui-state + Redis); no in-memory doubles. The suite's driver.py plays the client: it performs the real backend writes AND reports the outcomes to ui-state.
walking_skeleton.path: orgless principal → session_begin (awaiting_org_report) → probe GET /api/orgs/me (404) + report org_not_found (needs_org) → real POST /api/orgs + report org_created (ready) → automatic real POST /api/projects + report project_created (project_selected + phase=chat + active_scope.project_id) → engaged app entry on the POST's own response document (the (f) triple).
walking_skeleton.green_when: CDO-S1 + CDO-S2 are delivered (the closed-union happy vocabulary + ui-state report-driven realignment + the backend pure-resource contracts the dev happy path rides). CDO-S3..S5 add robustness/breadth, not the skeleton.

primary_target_env: AUTH_MODE=dev + DEV_NO_ORG=true (as shipped). Compose env deltas (ADR-048 §4) and the override AUTH_MODE-pin deletion land WITH DELIVER (CDO-S2/S5); DISTILL assumes the current stack.
mandate7_note: N/A in the usual sense: this is an HTTP-driven acceptance suite — the step layer imports only `driver` + `pytest`, never the production TS/Python modules. There are therefore no production-module scaffold stubs to create; RED-readiness is satisfied by the real stack returning pre-feature behaviour that fails the new-contract assertions. Collection is clean (no ImportError/ModuleNotFoundError → not BROKEN) and every test is RED-for-the-right-reason by construction (documented per file). Production-module scaffolding is DELIVER's concern in each language's unit suites.

slices.title: Walking skeleton: closed-union happy vocabulary + ui-state report-driven realignment (dev happy path, end to end)
slices.layer: shared + ui-state (driven by the client; backend dev behaviour already shipped)
slices.carpaccio: the thinnest vertical e2e slice — the first demoable dev happy path through every tier
slices.summary: Introduce the CLOSED ChatAppWireEvent union with the happy-path past-tense outcome members (session_begin kept; org_found/org_not_found/org_created; scope_resolved/no_projects_found/project_created) + the OrgSnapshot/ProjectSnapshot payloads, and realign the onboarding + project-context machines to transition on those reports: awaiting_org_report (replaces verifying; no I/O), needs_org→ready on org_created/org_found, awaiting_scope_report (replaces resolving_initial_scope), project_selected on project_created/scope_resolved. The parent's onSnapshot hand-offs (isUserReady, isInitialProjectSelected) are REUSED verbatim — only the producer of the child state changes from invoke-onDone to client report. State-document anonymous zero-states renamed. After this slice the dev happy path runs end to end with the suite's driver as the client.
acceptance_criteria: When the orgless principal begins a session via the ingress, onboarding settles in awaiting_org_report (no server-side probe).
When the client probes GET /api/orgs/me (404) and reports org_not_found, onboarding settles needs_org (phase onboarding).

When the client creates the org (real POST /api/orgs 201) and reports org_created {org:{id,name}}, onboarding settles ready and the parent advances to project_context (projectContext awaiting_scope_report).

When the client creates the default project (real POST /api/projects 201) and reports project_created {project:{id,name}}, projectContext settles project_selected, active_scope.project_id is non-null, and phase == chat — atomically on that POST's own response document (the (f) triple).
The returning-user fast path: org_found from awaiting_org_report settles ready directly.
Identity is seeded from verified headers at cold-start (context.user.email present in awaiting_org_report) — never written by any outcome event (INV-PCO / D4).
files_to_touch: shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts — closed union: add the happy outcome members + OrgSnapshot/ProjectSnapshot; retire org_form_submitted, create_project_submitted (UI-1 dies), switching_project_intent, the {type:string} catch-all (see CDO-S3 for the failure members + full closure).

shared/ui-state-wire/state-document.ts — region state renames (verifying→awaiting_org_report, resolving_initial_scope→awaiting_scope_report); anonymousStateDocument zero states; ChatAppPhase loses 'rejected' (CDO-S3).
ui-state/lib/machines/onboarding/machine.ts — realign per ADR-049 §4.2 (happy arms).
ui-state/lib/machines/project-context/machine.ts — realign per ADR-049 §5.1 (happy arms); Phase-D project_created accepted from awaiting_scope_report and no_projects.
ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/router.ts — ACL accepts the new happy members (full closed-union enforcement + unknown→400 in CDO-S3).
ui-state machine unit tests — report-driven transition tests replace invoke-onDone tests.
risks: The parent advance (login→engaged) must occur within the SAME settle as org_created so the org_created response already shows project_context/awaiting_scope_report — the suite asserts this. If the advance lags a tick, the walking skeleton's post-org assertion fails.

slices.title: Backend pure-resource contracts: OrgCreate name validation (422), availability endpoint, X-Provisioned-Org-Id, WorkOS shrink
slices.carpaccio: the resource SSOT half — the validation + read affordances the client's status→cause mapping and the auth-proxy interception depend on
slices.summary: Make the backend a pure resource store with the new contracts: OrgCreate gains name validation (strip + min length — the retired machine-side isOrgNameValid guard's new home; today name:str is unconstrained → 422 on blank); a NEW GET /api/orgs/availability?name= over get_organization_by_name (ADR-048 layer A); read X-Provisioned-Org-Id gated on trust_proxy_headers → repo id= (workos id IS the local id; absent → backend-generated); DELETE _create_workos_org + the auth_mode dispatch + WORKOS_* config + requires_reauth + ExternalServiceError. Name-uniqueness (409) + created_by are unchanged (already shipped).
acceptance_criteria: When the client POSTs a blank/whitespace org name, the backend returns 422 (the new OrgCreate validation), not 201.
A duplicate org name still returns the backend's JSON:API 409 (unchanged name-uniqueness).
Org create no longer auto-creates a project (regression lock — already shipped behaviour held).
GET /api/orgs/availability?name=<free> → 200 {available:true}; for a taken name → {available:false} (one route over get_organization_by_name).
POST /api/orgs honours X-Provisioned-Org-Id (gated on trust_proxy_headers) as the row id; absent → backend-generated id (dev).
The backend reads no AUTH_MODE and holds no WORKOS_* config (grep-clean at DELIVER).
driving_tests: backend/tests/use_cases/organization/test_create_organization.py (UPDATE: WorkOS-mode dispatch tests die; add provisioned-id + validation + availability tests — keeps the gate green)
files_to_touch: backend/app/routers/organizations.py — OrgCreate name validation (strip + min length); read X-Provisioned-Org-Id; NEW GET /api/orgs/availability.

backend/app/use_cases/organization/create_organization.py — DELETE _create_workos_org + mode dispatch + httpx import + requires_reauth; accept provisioned_org_id → repo id=; keep name-uniqueness + created_by.
backend/app/use_cases/organization/exceptions.py — DELETE ExternalServiceError (confirm sole consumer via grep).
backend/app/controllers/organization_controller.py — drop requires_reauth mapping; availability handler.
backend/app/config.py — DELETE auth_mode, workos_api_key, workos_api_url, workos_client_id, workos_redirect_uri.
backend/tests/use_cases/organization/ — rework per above.
risks: The WorkOS org-name-uniqueness ASSUMPTION (ADR-048 R1): if WorkOS DOES enforce uniqueness, compensation becomes mandatory-blocking — validate during this slice (the auth-proxy interception lands in CDO-S5; the backend half here is uniqueness-agnostic). Flagged HIGH in upstream-issues.md.

slices.title: Failure-class robustness + crash-class elimination + closed-union closure
slices.carpaccio: the no-dead-ends + no-crash guarantees — the failure and convergence arms layered onto the happy skeleton
slices.summary: Close the wire union (add org_create_failed{cause}, project_create_failed{cause}, scope_mismatch{cause}, project_switched + the cause enums; retire retry_clicked/create_project_clicked with the catch-all) and make every failure recoverable: org_create_failed splits re-edit (org_name_taken/org_name_invalid → remain needs_org with org_validation_error) vs retry (org_create_failed → report-accepting error_recoverable); project_create_failed → report-accepting error_recoverable; error_recoverable accepts every outcome report (partial-setup dies). Eliminate the settled-child crash class via phase-gated vocabulary routing: delete the root child_event total forward + active_child_id + user_rejected; onboarding vocabulary handled only on login, project-context vocabulary only on engaged; out-of-phase known events → no transition, current document returned (convergence); unknown type → 400 at the edge.
acceptance_criteria: A 409 reported as org_create_failed{cause:org_name_taken} keeps onboarding in needs_org with org_validation_error set; the person re-edits and succeeds (no dead end).
A retry-class org_create_failed{cause:org_create_failed} settles error_recoverable; a subsequent org_created settles ready (compensated/uncompensated indistinguishable; partial-setup gone).
project_create_failed settles projectContext error_recoverable; probe-first retry resolves to scope_resolved when the project already exists (no duplicate) or project_created otherwise.

A late/out-of-phase known event (e.g. org_created while phase=chat) causes NO transition and returns the current document; the ui-state process stays alive (subsequent request 200) — the 2026-06-10 crash regression.
An unknown event type is rejected with 400 at the edge in every phase (the closed union is total).
files_to_touch: shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts — full closure: cause enums + failure members; retire the remaining legacy members + catch-all.

shared/ui-state-wire/state-document.ts — ChatAppPhase loses 'rejected'; ReducedContext pruning (delete access_token, pending_project_name, most_recent_session_per_project, last_used_resolution_degraded — AR-7); keep org_validation_error/project_validation_error/retries_count.
ui-state/lib/machines/onboarding/{machine.ts, setup/domain.ts} — re-edit vs retry split; error_recoverable accepts reports; UnderlyingCauseTag drops partial-setup, adds the §c tags.
ui-state/lib/machines/project-context/machine.ts — error_recoverable accepts reports; scope_mismatch_terminal ← scope_mismatch; back_to_projects_clicked re-probe.
ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/machine.ts — delete root child_event/user_intent total forward, active_child_id, user_rejected; phase-gated vocabulary handlers on login/engaged/engaged.chat.
ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/setup/{actions.ts,guards.ts} — delete forwardChildEventToActiveChild, captureUserRejected, isUserRejected.

ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/router.ts — full closed-vocabulary Zod schema (z.ZodType<ChatAppWireEvent>), validated every POST; unknown→400; drop phase-split dispatch + switching_project_intent special case.
ui-state machine/router unit tests — the deterministic crash reproduction (send into a settled child / unknown-type 400) lives here (DWD-5).
risks: The crash reproduction proper (user_rejected via re-verify failure) is NOT reachable in the dev fake-WorkOS acceptance stack — the acceptance level asserts the convergence + liveness observable; the deterministic crash test is a ui-state unit test in this slice (DWD-5).

slices.title: auth-proxy mode discovery + reissue Set-Cookie (D8) + retry-KPI cleanup
slices.carpaccio: the sign-in-surface + session-continuity half — observable directly over HTTP
slices.summary: Register GET /api/auth/config → {mode} (side-effect-free, no credential, Cache-Control: public max-age=300) before the catch-all; extend applyOrgCreateReissue to append the two D1-attribute Set-Cookie headers (auth_token httpOnly + session=1) alongside the retained X-New-Access-Token on POST /api/orgs→201 (mode-agnostic; un-parks ui-cookie-session D8); retire the retry_clicked KPI trigger (the retry funnel re-derives from org_create.intercepted, AR-5).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/auth/config returns 200 {mode:'dev'} on the dev stack, with Cache-Control max-age, requiring no credential, and is side-effect-free (repeat calls identical; no CSRF state minted).
(workos-mode: returns {mode:'workos'} — auth-proxy unit + fake-WorkOS; not runnable in the dev acceptance stack.)
POST /api/orgs→201 with a user token carries Set-Cookie: auth_token=<jwt>; HttpOnly (+ session=1), never collapsed with the header, alongside X-New-Access-Token.
Dev path: Phase D succeeds with the same bearer (DEV_NO_ORG makes the reissue harmless redundancy) — no client token handling needed.
The retry_clicked inbound KPI trigger is removed (app.ts:703-709).
driving_tests: auth-proxy unit tests — workos-mode config, reissue cookie attributes, KPI trigger removal (gate-run)
files_to_touch: auth-proxy/app.ts — register GET /api/auth/config; extend applyOrgCreateReissue (Set-Cookie pair); retire retry_clicked KPI trigger.
auth-proxy/lib/cookies.ts — buildSetCookie reused (no change).
auth-proxy/test/ — config route, reissue-cookie assertions, KPI removal.
risks: The reissue hook must remain mode-agnostic so the dev acceptance can assert the Set-Cookie emission; do not gate it on AUTH_MODE!=dev (only the Secure attribute is dev-gated).

slices.title: ui/ client-drive surfaces + auth-proxy workos org-create interception
slices.carpaccio: the production client driver + the workos write path — completes the feature; the dev server-contracts are already proven by CDO-S1..S4 via the suite's driver
slices.summary: Build ui/app/lib/onboarding-driver.ts (the relocated flow policy: Phase-B probe→report with the definitive-answers-only rule, status→cause mapping, automatic Phase-D default project, the retry policies incl. probe-first convergence, initial-scope resolution ported from resolveInitialScopeFn); login.tsx mode-discovery consumption (no affordance until mode known; dev button only when mode=dev); onboarding.tsx (org form drives POST + reports; ProjectNameForm deleted; retry affordance; no raw cause tag rendered — friendly copy / generic surface); app-shell.tsx gate state-sets (rejected branch dies); backendClient ApiError {status,body}. auth-proxy lib/org-create-workflow.ts (NEW): pre-check availability → provision WorkOS org+membership → forward with X-Provisioned-Org-Id → compensate on failure; x-provisioned-org-id joins IDENTITY_HEADERS strip list. Session-chat egress retires under the same report pattern (DR-8/§e.5).
acceptance_criteria: ui/: the onboarding-driver probes, POSTs, maps status→cause, and posts the past-tense outcome reports, logging each event + region state via createLogger (browser/unit assertions).

ui/: login renders NO sign-in affordance until GET /api/auth/config resolves; dev button ONLY when mode=dev; a rendered failure shows friendly inline helper text (re-edit) or a generic 'something went wrong on our end' surface (retry) — NEVER a raw cause tag.

auth-proxy: in workos mode POST /api/orgs pre-checks availability, provisions the WorkOS org+membership, forwards with X-Provisioned-Org-Id, and compensates (DELETE) on a failed backend persist; a 409 pre-check never orphans an IdP org.
auth-proxy: x-provisioned-org-id is stripped from inbound client requests on every route (strip-then-inject).
session-chat egress is retired (report-driven vocabulary per §e.5).
driving_tests: Server contracts already covered by CDO-S1..S4 acceptance scenarios (the suite's driver is the client).
ui/ DELIVER unit tests — onboarding-driver policy (pure, no browser): status→cause mapping, probe-first convergence, definitive-answers-only, createLogger narration.
auth-proxy unit tests + fake-WorkOS acceptance (via the WORKOS_BASE pin, ADR-048 R4) — interception: pre-check, provision, carry header, compensate, timeouts (NOT this python suite — §e.4).
(optional) a browser-pass assertion that the console-log audit narration exists (createLogger).
files_to_touch: ui/app/lib/onboarding-driver.ts — NEW.
ui/app/auth/bootstrap.ts — fetchAuthConfig() (memoized, Zod at the boundary).
ui/app/routes/login.tsx — mode-discovery consumption.
ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx — POST+report; ProjectNameForm deleted; retry affordance; friendly copy (no raw tags).
ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx — gate state-sets; rejected branch dies; probe trigger after bootstrap.
ui/app/catalog/dataSources/backendClient.ts — ApiError {status,body}.
auth-proxy/lib/org-create-workflow.ts — NEW (pre-check/provision/forward/compensate + ADR-048 §5 events).
auth-proxy/lib/user-auth/workos.ts — org-provisioning ops (create/membership/delete; 5s timeouts).
auth-proxy/lib/auth.ts — add x-provisioned-org-id to IDENTITY_HEADERS.
auth-proxy/app.ts — insert the interception branch in app.all('*').
ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/{machine.ts, setup/actors.ts} — retire egress; report-only vocabulary (§e.5).
shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts — SessionChatWireEvent members (§e.5).
docker-compose.yml + docker-compose.override.yml — env deltas (ADR-048 §4) + delete the api AUTH_MODE pin.
risks: This slice carries the single-cut closed-union deploy (shared + ui-state + ui/ deploy together in compose — ADR-050 cost note). Sequence the env deltas + override pin deletion HERE so the stack is coherent. The workos-mode coverage is auth-proxy unit + fake-WorkOS, not the python acceptance suite.
